reasoning: The informal statement says "a monic polynomial of fourth degree" without specifying the coefficient ring; the natural mathematical reading is over ℝ (or at least ℚ). The Lean formalization restricts to `Polynomial ℤ`, which forces the (unspecified) fourth root to be an integer. There exist monic degree-4 polynomials over ℚ/ℝ satisfying f(1)=10, f(2)=20, f(3)=30 that are NOT in ℤ[x] (e.g., (x-1)(x-2)(x-3)(x-1/2) + 10x). So the ℝ version is strictly more general — it universally quantifies over a larger class of polynomials — and the ℤ version is logically implied by it. The Mathlib encodings of `Monic`, `degree = 4`, and `eval` are correct, and the conclusion matches. The only issue is the narrowed coefficient ring, which makes the Lean statement logically weaker than the most natural reading of the informal problem.
